name: EventEase CI/CD Pipeline

# This workflow triggers on pushes and pull requests to the main branch
on:
  push:
    branches: [ "main", "master" ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ "main", "master" ]
  # Allows you to run this workflow manually from the Actions tab
  workflow_dispatch:

# Environment variables available to all jobs
env:
  DOTNET_VERSION: '8.0.x'

jobs:
  # JOB 1: Build and Test
  build-and-test:
    name: Build and Test Application
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
    # Step 1: Check out the code from GitHub
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    # Step 2: Setup .NET SDK
    - name: Setup .NET
      uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
      with:
        dotnet-version: ${{ env.DOTNET_VERSION }}
    
    # Step 3: Restore NuGet packages
    - name: Restore dependencies
      run: dotnet restore
      working-directory: ./
    
    # Step 4: Build the application
    - name: Build application
      run: dotnet build --no-restore --configuration Release
      working-directory: ./
    
    # Step 5: Run tests (if you have any)
    - name: Run tests
      run: dotnet test --no-build --verbosity normal
      working-directory: ./
      continue-on-error: true  # Don't fail the workflow if no tests exist yet
    
    # Step 6: Publish the application
    - name: Publish application
      run: dotnet publish --configuration Release --output ./publish
      working-directory: ./
    
    # Step 7: Upload build artifacts (optional - for deployment later)
    - name: Upload build artifacts
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: eventease-build
        path: ./publish
        retention-days: 7
